pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 19: TARGET_URL="http://localhost:8080/InterpreterBooth42"; verifier.py line 56 checks for room_name in final_url
task.json description specifies 'Enter the display name Interpreter_ES'; verifier.py line 66 validates displayName against expected_name
GitHub issue #13820 (jitsi/jitsi-meet) confirms soundsParticipantJoined is a real Jitsi Meet settings key that exists in the Redux state 'features/base/settings'
GitHub issue #13820 (jitsi/jitsi-meet) confirms soundsParticipantLeft is a real Jitsi Meet settings key
GitHub issue #13820 (jitsi/jitsi-meet) confirms soundsReactions is a real Jitsi Meet settings key; also referenced in issues #13420 about reaction sounds and Bluetooth earphones

GitHub issue #13820 (jitsi/jitsi-meet) confirms soundsTalkWhileMuted is a real Jitsi Meet settings key; commit 11f2e7291e in git.home.packden.us/decentralize/jitsi-meet mentions 'Talk while muted sound notification'; feature documented in Jitsi blog
setup_task.sh line 11 waits for 'http://localhost:8080' HTTP service; setup_jitsi.sh line 84 confirms 'Jitsi Meet is running at http://localhost:8080'

verifier.py lines 82-114 expect these exact keys (soundsParticipantJoined, soundsParticipantLeft, soundsReactions, soundsTalkWhileMuted) with boolean values in the settings object retrieved from Redux store

privileged_info_summary: The task 'configure_notification_sounds@1' is a synthetic GUI interaction task for Jitsi Meet. The metadata specifies three real Jitsi Meet setting keys (soundsParticipantJoined, soundsParticipantLeft, soundsReactions, soundsTalkWhileMuted) that control notification sound behaviors, all confirmed to exist in Jitsi Meet's 'features/base/settings' Redux state. The room name is 'InterpreterBooth42', the expected display name is 'Interpreter_ES', and the local Jitsi Meet instance runs at http://localhost:8080. All data is synthetic with no external datasets involved. The task requires an agent to configure these four sound settings to specific values (three disabled, one enabled) through the Jitsi Meet Settings UI.
